title: No license — 9,611 forks in legal gray zone
description: No LICENSE file. All rights reserved by default. 9,611 forks technically have no legal right to redistribute. Karpathy likely intends permissive use but hasn't formalized.

title: 173 open issues, mostly unaddressed community ports
description: Issues are primarily requests for Mac/AMD/smaller GPU support. Karpathy is intentionally not absorbing these — wants forks, not PRs.
recommendation: None — this is the intended distribution model.

title: Zero tests, zero CI
description: No test files. No CI workflows (Copilot code review only, all failures). Agent crash recovery is the only resilience mechanism.
recommendation: Appropriate for research artifact. Not appropriate if adopted as infrastructure.

title: No input validation on agent-modified train.py
description: Agent has full write access to train.py. No sandbox, no validation. By design — the agent IS the researcher.
recommendation: None for current use case. Sandbox if exposing to untrusted agents.

title: POSITIVE: 3-file architecture — masterclass in constraint-driven design
description: prepare.py (immutable, 389 lines) + train.py (agent-editable, 630 lines) + program.md (instructions, 114 lines). Clear contracts: what's fixed, what's editable, what's the instruction set.

title: POSITIVE: program.md is the most effective agent instruction file in any analyzed repo
description: 114 lines define: setup protocol, experimentation loop, keep/discard binary, crash recovery, NEVER STOP autonomy, simplicity criterion, logging convention. Complete autonomous research organization in Markdown.

title: POSITIVE: Fixed 5-min budget makes experiments comparable
description: TIME_BUDGET=300 in prepare.py. Every experiment runs exactly 5 minutes regardless of architecture changes. ~12 experiments/hour, ~100 overnight. Directly comparable results.
recommendation: Apply pattern to time-bounded agent work in JASON-OS.

title: POSITIVE: Simplicity criterion encoded in agent instructions
description: program.md explicitly instructs: 'removing something and getting equal or better results is a great outcome.' Agent optimizes for metric AND simplicity simultaneously.
recommendation: Consider encoding similar criteria in skill instructions.
